{{Quote|Capital warships serve many functions: exploration, transportation, tourism, and—especially in recent times—war. Even small fleets, like those of the Rebel Alliance, have at least one or two capital ships.|Elpfel|<ref name="SETSB" />}}
'''Elpfel''' was a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] with [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and mottled brown skin who served as an [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Fleet]], holding the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Commander/Legends|commander]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] fought between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Elpful was the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] ''[[Wavesong]]'' and wore a beige [[Mon Calamari Crewman uniform]].<ref name="SETSB" />

At some point, Elpfel talked about [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], including Imperial ones, which the Mon Calamari described as "the most awesome [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] of war ever constructed" with their heavy [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating, high-powered [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]], and enormous amounts of firepower making them capable of destroying [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. However, Elpfel considered it lucky for [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] that the Imperial capital [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were not unstoppable, mentioning their high power consumption, low maneuverability, complex redundant system design, large crew requirements, and tactical disadvantages against small [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] as flaws.<ref name="SETSB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Elpfel was introduced in [[1997]]'s ''[[Star Wars Trilogy Sourcebook, Special Edition]]'', a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="SETSB" />
